content.rendered:
[vc_row][vc_column][vc_empty_space height=”50px” el_class=”height30″][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row el_class=”flexrow”][vc_column width=”1/4″ el_class=”flextwo gt-custom-sidebar-column”][vc_widget_sidebar sidebar_id=”honeymoon-package-international-sidebar”][/vc_column][vc_column width=”3/4″ el_class=”second-div-text flexone”][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

Seychelles is a beautiful island famous for its pristine white beaches, emerald green oceans, and virgin coral reefs. The island has always been an exclusive destination loved by discerning travellers looking to escape the humdrum of daily life. Thanks to ATW Travels, you can also travel to this dream destination for your honeymoon. Call us to choose from a range of Seychelles honeymoon packages that won’t break the bank. We’ll help you explore the island and ensure you have the best time of your life. Our packages are designed to provide a memorable vacation for every couple. The experience includes accommodation at the most luxurious resorts, travel, transportation, sightseeing, and adventure activities.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Why Visit Seychelles?” use_theme_fonts=”yes”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

Seychelles is an archipelago made of 115 tiny islands, most of them either coral beds or made of granite. Eight of these islands are inhabited, with Mahe being the largest and the most populous island. This country boasts some of the best beaches in the world, with the Anse Source D’Argent being the most photographed beach in the world. It is also home to two UNESCO world heritage sites – Aldabra, the second largest coral atoll in the world, and Valle de Mai, a secluded but picturesque location.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

Seychelles has some of the best flora and fauna, including the rare black parrot, giant tortoise, and coco de mer. It is also home to a rich and biodiverse marine life, making it one of the best diving sights in the world.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_single_image image=”26222″ img_size=”full” alignment=”center”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Places Included in Our Seychelles Honeymoon Packages” use_theme_fonts=”yes”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

Our romantic Seychelles honeymoon tour package includes all major destinations, including Mahe and Praslin Island. Here are a few attractions you mustn’t miss while in Seychelles:

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Praslin Island” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left” use_theme_fonts=”yes”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

Praslin is the second largest island in Seychelles. It is 4 km wide and 11km long. It is home to some of the best attractions, including Anse Lazio Beach, Anse Georgette Beach, and the Valle De Mai Nature Reserve. The reserve is a palm forest famous for the notorious Coco De Mer – the largest nut in the world and believed to have been consumed by Eve in the Garden of Eden.

Praslin Island is also famous for its rare black pearls harvested in the local waters. Snorkelling is a popular activity on the island – if you’d love to spot some healthy coral reefs and beautiful marine life, remember to discuss it while booking Seychelles honeymoon packages. You can also spend your days relaxing on the beaches or enjoying some exciting water sports.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_single_image image=”26223″ img_size=”full” alignment=”center”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Mahe Island” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left” use_theme_fonts=”yes”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

The largest island in Seychelles, Mahe is also home to a beautiful mountain range and a rich bounty of flora and fauna. Grab a picnic basket and spend your day riding bikes or hiking on the beautiful mountain trails. You can also consider ocean snorkeling at Beau Vallon.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”La Digue Island” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left” use_theme_fonts=”yes”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

The island is home to some of the most serene and breathtakingly beautiful beaches in Seychelles, like Petite Anse and Anse Source D’Argent. La Digue is also famous for its surfing scene. The Felicite Island and the Veuve Nature Reserve are a part of this beautiful island – we always include it in our romantic Seychelles honeymoon tour package.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Curieuse Island” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left” use_theme_fonts=”yes”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

The Curieuse Island is perfect for honeymooners looking to explore the place and learn more about the island’s tortoises, green sea turtles, and takamaka trees. It is also famous for its bare red earth and the coco de mer. Don’t miss a visit to the Marine National Park – the bio reserve is home to endemic plants and birds. Make sure a visit to Curieuse is included in This destination is a must-see in your romantic Seychelles honeymoon tour package.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_single_image image=”26223″ img_size=”full” alignment=”center”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Silhouette Island” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left” use_theme_fonts=”yes”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

This is the third-largest island in Seychelles and is definitely worth visiting. From couple spa treatments and diving to fishing, there are plenty of activities for young couples.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Activities Included in Your Seychelles Honeymoon Packages:” use_theme_fonts=”yes”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

  • You can go island-hopping with your loved one on the many causeways that have been built across the islands.
  • Consider soaking in the glorious sunrise and sunset on one of the many pristine white sandy beaches in Seychelles.
  • Don’t miss making a couple of spa appointments at one of the many resorts on the islands.
  • The island country is the best place for snorkelling, scuba diving, surfing, canoe riding, and other water sports activities at the beaches.
  • Spend time lazing around on the beach.
  • You could also take a romantic walk on the sandy beaches with the glistening waters in the backdrop.
  • Visit the various nature parks that have preserved the flora and fauna of the land.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_single_image image=”26224″ img_size=”full” alignment=”center”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Accommodation Options in Our Seychelles Honeymoon Tour Packages” use_theme_fonts=”yes”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

Seychelles has the finest resorts for honeymoon couples. At ATW Travels, we’ve curated some of the best Seychelles packages for couples that fit any budget and travel style. Resort rates may vary from Rs.20,000 per night and go up to Rs. 200,000 per night. Some of the top resorts in Seychelles include:

  • Four Seasons Resort, Mahe, Seychelles.
  • Constance Ephilia Resort, Mahe, Seychelles.
  • Story Seychelles.
  • Hilton Seychelles Labris Resorts & Spa.
  • Raffles Seychelles.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner full_width=”stretch_row_content_no_spaces” css=”.vc_custom_1645675083319{margin-bottom: 20px !important;padding-top: 40px !important;padding-bottom: 40px !important;background-color: #1a2b48 !important;}” el_id=”mobileshoecontact” el_class=”show-mobile”][vc_column_inner el_class=”whitecontent” width=”1/2″][vc_column_text css=”.vc_custom_1646219943195{margin-bottom: 0px !important;}”]

[/vc_column_text][vc_icon icon_fontawesome=”fas fa-phone” background_style=”rounded” size=”xs” align=”center” el_class=”gthomeicon” css=”.vc_custom_1643786099141{margin-bottom: 0px !important;}”][vc_column_text]

[/vc_column_text][vc_separator el_width=”30″][vc_icon icon_fontawesome=”fas fa-envelope” background_style=”rounded” size=”xs” align=”center” el_class=”gthomeicon” css=”.vc_custom_1643786244533{margin-bottom: 0px !important;}”][vc_column_text]

info@atwtravels.com

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][vc_column_inner el_class=”whitecontent” width=”1/2″ css=”.vc_custom_1644654313324{border-left-width: 1px !important;border-left-color: #ffffff !important;border-left-style: solid !important;}”][vc_column_text css=”.vc_custom_1653551666460{margin-bottom: 0px !important;}”]

Book Your Dream Vacay Today!

[/vc_column_text]

[/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Reaching Seychelles and Getting Around the Islands” use_theme_fonts=”yes”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

The Republic of Seychelles has six major airports, but the Seychelles International Airport at Victoria is the busiest, followed by the Praslin Island Domestic Airport. There are a few direct flights from India to Mahe and Praslin Island. When in Seychelles, consider hiring a bike. You could also travel around through ferry boats, catamarans, taxicabs, and helicopters.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_single_image image=”26225″ img_size=”full” alignment=”center”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”What To Buy in Seychelles?” use_theme_fonts=”yes”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

You would be spoilt for choices while picking souvenirs at one of the island’s many markets. Some of the popular products you can buy include:

  • Coconut shell artifacts are famous on the island. You could also buy ones carved like the Coco De Mer.
  • Seychelles is home to high-quality vanilla pods found abundantly on the island.
  • The rare black pearl is widely available in Seychelles. You could buy individual pearls or pearl jewelry.

There are plenty of local marketplaces that offer a range of local produce and art items:

  • Sir Selwyn Clarke Market for local souvenirs and spices.
  • Victoria Market for sarongs, flavored oils, and artwork.
  • Bazar Labrin, if you love handcrafted items.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”What To Eat in Seychelles?” use_theme_fonts=”yes”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

Like any tropical island, Seychelles is famous for seafood and tropical fruits and vegetables. Indulge in fantastic grilled seafood platters, exotic fruit punches, mocktails, and cocktails. In addition, the island has some interesting alcoholic drinks which are a rarity, like the Takamaka Bay Rum, Kalou (fermented wine infused with coconut tree flowers), and Buka ( Sugarcane Rum made at home).

Spend your first vacation with your beloved in this tropical magic destination and get your holiday designed by the experts at ATW Travels. We will ensure that your Seychelles trip package for couples is the best and the most memorable. Get to know about our daily offers by visiting our Instagram Profile.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

Seychelles Family Adventure

Seychelles, Praslin

Duration

[/vc_column_text][vc_column_text]
